overview: safeT PTT supplements your existing Land Mobile Radio (LMR) network — it does not replace mission-critical conventional or trunked radio. Bridges and codecs let dispatch monitor legacy radio traffic on safeT channels while field units use LTE handsets.
disclaimer: Agencies should maintain conventional LMR backup for life-safety operations. safeT is designed for encrypted LTE push-to-talk, dispatch oversight, and optional ingest from existing radio feeds.

paths.title: Stream URL bridge
paths.summary: Ingest audio from an HTTP stream — Icecast, HLS, or MP3 — onto a safeT channel. The server runs ffmpeg and VOX-gates traffic so dispatch hears conventional radio alongside LTE units.
details: Configure in safeT Control → Bridges with source type stream URL.
Server-side ingest — no desktop app required once the stream is live.
Ideal for scanner feeds, trunk-recorder outputs, or rebroadcast mounts.
paths.audience: Comm centers monitoring legacy talkgroups in Command

paths.title: Audio device bridge
paths.summary: Capture line-in from a physical radio or scanner on a Windows PC and key it onto a safeT channel. Bidirectional bridges can also decode safeT traffic back to a speaker or radio input.
details: Run from the web console Bridges page or the standalone safeT Bridge desktop app.
safeT Bridge is designed for unattended bridge boxes — local UI survives server redeploys.
Adjust input gain, VOX threshold, and hang time per bridge with live meters.
paths.audience: Agencies with a dedicated PC wired to a base station or scanner

paths.title: P25 trunk ingest (SDR tooling)
paths.summary: For P25 trunked systems, safeT does not decode RF directly. Use trunk-recorder plus the sdr-bridge tooling in this repo to emit one Icecast stream per talkgroup, then attach each mount as a stream URL bridge.
details: One RTL-SDR + trunk-recorder can feed multiple talkgroups as separate HTTP streams.
Each talkgroup maps to one safeT channel via its own stream bridge.
RF bandwidth limits apply — wide-spread voice channels may need additional SDR sources.
paths.audience: Technical staff integrating county trunked LMR monitor feeds

codecs.title: Voice codecs
codecs.intro: Channels can be configured for different vocoders. Clients auto-negotiate per channel settings.

items.name: P25 IMBE
items.body: APCO P25–compatible IMBE vocoder for radio-literate deployments and interoperability testing with P25-oriented workflows.

items.name: Codec2 3200
items.body: Low-bitrate open vocoder for bandwidth-constrained LTE links.

items.name: Opus
items.body: Modern wideband codec for clear voice on phones and dispatch soft-radio.

items.name: Cross-agency mutual aid channels
items.note: Share selected talkgroups across agencies — in development; contact sales for early access.

items.name: Hardware LMR gateway appliance
items.note: Turn-key radio gateway — today use stream URL or audio device bridges.
